km.France: ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.lifeSummary: 當Guy II Le Mesureur於1330年出生在法國時，他的父親Guy le Mesureur40歲，母親Agnès de Merlin40歲。他和Le Mesureur至少有1個兒子。他去世於1370年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guy II Le Mesureur于1330年出生在法国时，他的父亲Guy le Mesureur40岁，母亲Agnès de Merlin40岁。他和Le Mesureur至少有1个儿子。他去世于1370年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Guy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of a compound name starting with witu ‘wood’ or wīt ‘wide’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England. In Old French initial w- regularly became gu-. The usual Norman forms of the name were Gy or Guido. In medieval Latin the same name is found as Wido. It was a popular name among the Normans, enhanced no doubt by the romance of Guy of Warwick, recounting the exploits of a folk hero of the Crusades.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Guy II Le Mesureur was born in 1330, in France, his father, Guy le Mesureur, was 40 and his mother, Agnès de Merlin, was 40. He had at least 1 son with Le Mesureur. He died after 1370.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guy II Le Mesureur est né en 1330, à France, son père, Guy le Mesureur, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Agnès de Merlin, avait 40 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Le Mesureur. Il est décédé après 1370.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guy II Le Mesureur 1330 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guy le Mesureur 40 und seine Mutter Agnès de Merlin 40. Er hatte mit Le Mesureur mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb nach 1370.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guy II Le Mesureur è nato il nel 1330 nel Francia, il suo padre, Guy le Mesureur, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Agnès de Merlin, aveva 40 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Le Mesureur. Morì dopo il 1370.

ja.lifeSummary: Guy II Le Mesureurが1330年にフランスで生まれた時，父Guy le Mesureurは40歳，母Agnès de Merlinは40歳であった。彼はLe Mesureurとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1370年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guy II Le Mesureur(이)가 1330년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guy le Mesureur(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnès de Merlin(은)는 40세였다. 그는 Le Mesureur(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1370년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guy II Le Mesureur nasceu em 1330, em França, seu pai, Guy le Mesureur, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Agnès de Merlin, tinha 40 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Le Mesureur. Ele faleceu após 1370.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guy II Le Mesureur родился в 1330 г. в местности Франция, его отцу по имени Guy le Mesureur было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnès de Merlin, 40. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Le Mesureur. Он умер после 1370 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guy II Le Mesureur nació en 1330, en Francia, su papá, Guy le Mesureur, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Agnès de Merlin, tenía 40 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Le Mesureur. Murió después de 1370.
